instruction: Use TimelineView to create a high-frequency updating clock that only redraws the minimal subview necessary, avoiding unnecessary parent view updates.
Demonstrate how to use Canvas for efficient, custom drawing of a real-time waveform, updating only when new data arrives.
Show how to use .performanceSensitive() to optimize a SwiftUI view that animates a large number of moving particles.
Implement a view that uses EquatableView to avoid unnecessary updates when only a subset of properties change.
Demonstrate how to use @StateObject and @ObservedObject correctly to minimize view recomputation in a complex dashboard.
Show how to use .drawingGroup(opaque:colorMode:) for offscreen rendering to optimize a complex composited SwiftUI view.
Implement a TimelineView that only updates a subview when a specific model property changes, using @Binding and computed properties.
Show how to use .transaction(_:) to disable implicit animations for a specific SwiftUI update, improving performance.
Demonstrate how to use PreferenceKey and GeometryReader to optimize layout calculations and avoid redundant view updates.
Show how to use .background(Material.ultraThin) efficiently in a scrolling list without causing excessive redraws.
Implement a custom ViewModifier that applies .performanceSensitive() only when a certain threshold of updates per second is exceeded.
Show how to use .performanceSensitive() in a SwiftUI chart with thousands of data points to maintain smooth scrolling and zooming.
